questions.description: This decides the public command shape and how discoverable profiles feel relative to skills.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Put profiles inside the skills family as `afk skills profiles ...`. Profiles operate on skills and should feel like skill-management mode, not a separate top-level AFK concept yet.

questions.prompt: How should activating a profile affect installed skills?
questions.description: This is the most important safety decision: whether AFK physically moves skill folders or records state elsewhere.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use the same enable/disable mechanics already used by AFK: activating a profile moves non-profile global skills into `.disabled`, and deactivating restores the pre-profile active set from an AFK state file. V1 should target the shared global library only unless `--agent` support is explicitly added later.

questions.prompt: How should skills survive every active profile?
questions.description: Examples might include routing, AFK core workflow, or emergency utility skills that should never disappear during focus mode.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Add an explicit `alwaysOn` list in `profiles.json`. Active profile skills plus always-on skills remain enabled; everything else is temporarily disabled. Avoid an implicit default profile for V1 because it hides too much behavior and makes troubleshooting harder.

questions.description: This defines the durable catalog file users may sync later.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use `~/.agents/afk/catalog/profiles.json` with top-level `activeProfileId`, top-level `alwaysOn`, and `items` containing `{ id, name, skills }`. Keep state needed to restore folders in a separate transient file, not inside the user-editable profiles catalog.

questions.prompt: Which profile commands should be implemented now?
questions.description: This keeps V1 shippable while still useful.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Implement `list`, `show`, `create`, `delete`, `activate`, `deactivate`, and `status`. Use interactive searchable skill selection for create/edit where possible. Skip profile edit/rename/import/export unless needed after V1.

questions.prompt: What safety boundaries should profile activation obey?
questions.description: This determines how AFK avoids accidentally hiding or overwriting skills.
questions.recommendedAnswer: V1 should never delete skills, should refuse activation when another profile state is active unless forced/deactivated first, should dry-run all folder moves, should preserve pre-existing disabled skills as disabled after deactivate, and should only operate on global shared skills.
